6.90582 13.9262 6.6528 13.3467L5.99456 11.8391C5.40878 10.4975 4.35442 9.42945 3.03922 8.84565L1.22743 8.04143C0.651402 7.78575 0.651402 6.94776 1.22743 6.69207L2.98264 5.91296C4.33165 5.31414 5.40493 17.0174C13.9855 17.328 14.4155 17.328 14.5511 17.0174ZM13.7809 14.2852L14.2028 13.8662L14.6159 14.2852L14.2028 14.6924L13.7809 14.2852Z" fill="#525252"></path></svg>Open in<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-chevron-down text-tertiary" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m6 9 6 6 6-6"></path></svg></button></div></div></div><p>This endpoint retrieves the results of asynchronous eligibility checks you submitted through the asynchronous <a href="/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-batch-eligibility">Batch Eligibility Checks</a> endpoint or through <a href="/docs/healthcare/batch-refresh-eligibility-checks#manual-submission">CSV upload</a> in the Stedi portal. It doesn&#x27;t return results for real-time eligibility checks.</p>
<ol>
<li>Call this endpoint. You can optionally add one or more query parameters to filter the results you want to retrieve.</li>
<li>The endpoint returns completed checks matching the criteria. Stedi retries checks that fail due to <a href="/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-connectivity-issues">payer connectivity issues</a> for 8 - 24 hours. It can take up to the configured retry period for all checks in a batch to return results.</li>
</ol>
<p>Each <code>item</code> in the response contains the benefits information for a completed eligibility check. Note that our documentation lists all enums officially allowed in the eligibility response. Some payers return non-compliant values, which Stedi passes through as is.</p>
<h2 class="flex scroll-m-28 flex-row items-center gap-2" id="pagination"><a data-card="" href="#pagination" class="peer">Pagination</a><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link size-3.5 shrink-0 text-fd-muted-foreground opacity-0 transition-opacity peer-hover:opacity-100" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></h2>
<p>By default, the response includes the results for up to 10 eligibility checks within a single page - each eligibility response is represented as one item in the <code>items</code> array. You can control the number of results returned per page using the <code>pageSize</code> query parameter, which accepts a value between 1 and 200.</p>
<p>When there are additional pages of results, the response includes the <code>nextPageToken</code> property. To retrieve the next page of results, call the endpoint with the same <code>batchId</code> and other query parameters, and set the <code>pageToken</code> query parameter to the <code>nextPageToken</code> value. Repeat this process until the response doesn&#x27;t include a <code>nextPageToken</code> property.</p>
<p>If you set the page size to a value &gt; 20, Stedi returns the requested batch check results in <code>gzip</code> format to reduce the size. Many common HTTP clients accept <code>gzip</code> by default, but if not, you must add the <code>Accept-Encoding: gzip</code> header to your request and resubmit.</p><div class="flex items-end class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M7.78428 14L8.2047 10H4V8H8.41491L8.94043 3H10.9514L10.4259 8H14.4149L14.9404 3H16.9514L16.4259 8H20V10H16.2157L15.7953 14H20V16H15.5851L15.0596 21H13.0486L13.5741 16H9.58509L9.05957 21H7.04855L7.57407 16H4V14H7.78428ZM9.7953 14H13.7843L14.2047 10H10.2157L9.7953 14Z"></path></svg>Integer</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Range: <!-- -->≥ 1 and ≤ 200</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The maximum number of check results to return in a page - each check is represented as one item in the <code>items</code> array. If not specified, the default is 10.</p>
<ul>
<li>If you set the page size to a value &gt; 20, Stedi returns the requested batch check results in <code>gzip</code> format to reduce the size. Many common HTTP clients accept <code>gzip</code> by default, but if not, you must add the <code>Accept-Encoding: gzip</code> header to your request and resubmit.</li>
<li>When check results are especially large, Stedi may return fewer than the requested number per page. In these cases, you can use the <code>nextPageToken</code> property to retrieve the rest of the requested results.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3 py-4 first:border-t-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A token returned by a previous call to this operation in the <code>nextPageToken</code> property. If not specified, Stedi returns the first page of results.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3 py-4 first:border-t-0 text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An identifier for a batch of eligibility checks submitted through the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-batch-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Batch Eligibility Check</a> endpoint. Use this to retrieve results for eligibility checks in the batch.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3 py-4 class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Format: <!-- -->date-time</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An ISO 8601 formatted string. For example <code>2023-08-28T00:00:00Z</code>. Stedi returns asynchronous eligibility checks that have completed processing after this time. Completed means that the payer has successfully returned a benefits response, the check has failed due to errors in the request, or that the payer has been unavailable for 8 hours and Stedi will no longer attempt to retry.</p></div></div></div><nav class="api-section-nav"><div class="flex gap-4 items-center"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Each eligibility check response is included as a separate item in this array. The response shape is identical to the shape of the response for the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Real-Time Eligibility Check</a> endpoint, with the addition of two new properties that help you correlate the results with individual eligibility checks.
- <code>batchId</code> contains the <code>batchId</code> Stedi returned from the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-batch-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Batch Eligibility Check</a> endpoint when making the request.
- <code>submitterTransactionIdentifier</code> contains the unique identifier for the eligibility check that you submitted in the request.</p><details leading-[24px]"><p>The name Stedi uses when displaying this batch on the <a href="https://portal.stedi.com/app/healthcare/checks/batch" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Eligibility check batches page</a>. If you didn&#x27;t specify a name when submitting the batch, this is the same as the <code>batchId</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the patient&#x27;s healthcare benefits, such as coverage level (individual vs. family), coverage type (deductibles, co-pays, etc.), out of pocket maximums, and more.</p>
<p>Payers typically return at least the following properties: <code>code</code>, <code>coverageLevelCode</code>, <code>serviceTypeCodes</code>, and either <code>benefitAmount</code> or <code>benefitPercent</code>. However, the exact properties returned in this object are up to the payer&#x27;s discretion.</p>
<p>The payer may send benefits information for service type codes (STCs) you didn&#x27;t request - this is expected. The STC you send in the request tells the payer the types of benefits information you want, but they aren&#x27;t required to respond with exactly the same STC(s) in the response. Receiving different STCs than you requested can also mean that the payer is ignoring the STC you sent, which is why we recommend <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-stc-procedure-codes#test-payer-stc-support" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">testing payers</a> to determine their support for specific STCs.</p>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Determine patient benefits</a> for more information about benefit types, details about how to interpret the <code>benefitsInformation</code> array, and additional examples.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating whether the benefit is subject to prior authorization or certification. Can be <code>Y</code> - Yes, <code>N</code> - No, or <code>U</code> - Unknown.</p>
<ul>
<li>When this property is set to <code>U</code>, it means the payer can&#x27;t determine in real time whether prior authorization is required for the service. They may require additional details, like diagnosis or place of service to make a determination. Check the <code>additionalInformation[].description</code> property for any additional clarification. You can also contact the payer directly or use their provider portal to get more information.</li>
<li>Some payers may send additional notes about prior authorization rules in the <code>additionalInformation[].description</code> property. Payers may also send prior authorization notes in a separate <code>benefitsInformation</code> object with a code of <code>1</code> (Active coverage), <code>CB</code> (Coverage Benefit), or <code>D</code> (Benefit Description).</li>
</ul>
<p>Visit our <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-network-status-authorization-referrals#is-prior-authorization-required" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient benefits docs</a> to learn more about prior authorization.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>N</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>U</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>Y</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3" 14.8284L14.8282 7.75736Z"></path></svg></span></span></a><div class="text-[12px] font-[400] text-tertiary font-mono break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitAmount</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The monetary benefit amount, such as a patient&#x27;s co-pay or deductible. This value is expressed as a decimal, such as 100.00.</p>
<p>The payer will always send a value in this property when the <code>benefitsInformation[].code</code> = <code>B</code> - Co-Payment, <code>C</code> - Deductible, <code>G</code> - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), <code>J</code> - Cost Containment, or <code>Y</code> - Spend Down. For those codes, this value represents the patient&#x27;s portion of responsibility.</p>
<p>The payer will <strong>never</strong> send this value when <code>benefitsInformation[].code</code> = <code>A</code> - Co-Insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility.</p>
<p>Learn more about <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient costs</a>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitPercent</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The percentage of the benefit, such as co-insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility.</p>
<p>The payer will always send a value in this property when <code>benefitsInformation[].code</code> = <code>A</code> - Co-Insurance. For this code, this value represents the patient&#x27;s portion of the responsibility. The percentage is expressed as a decimal, such as <code>0.80</code> represents 80%.</p>
<p>The payer will <strong>never</strong> send a value in this property when <code>benefitsInformation[].code</code> = <code>B</code> - Co-Payment, <code>C</code> - Deductible, <code>G</code> - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), <code>J</code> - Cost Containment, or <code>Y</code> - Spend Down.</p>
<p>Learn more about <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient costs</a>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The family unit number. This is returned when the payer is a pharmacy benefits manager (PBM) and the patient has a suffix to their member ID number that is used in the NCPDP Telecom Standard Insurance Segment, in field <code>303-C3</code> (Person Code). For all other uses, the family unit number (suffix) is considered part of the patient&#x27;s member ID number.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitsAdditionalInformation.hicNumber</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation</a>, the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t benefits.</p>
<ul>
<li>This is where you can find benefit-specific eligibility dates, if provided. These dates override dates provided in <code>planDateInformation</code> for this benefit type.</li>
<li>This is where the payer may specify the last time the service was rendered (<code>latestVisitOrConsultation</code>), which you can use to determine whether the patient has already reached the allowed frequency, if applicable. For example, this object could contain the date when the patient received their last dental cleaning.</li>
<li>These dates only apply to the <code>benefitsInformation</code> object in which this <code>benefitsDateInformation</code> is provided.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Other entities associated with the eligibility or benefits. This could be a provider, an individual, an organization, or another payer. When present, this array typically contains information about the patient&#x27;s primary care provider (PCP), another organization that handles a specific benefit type (such as telehealth mental health services), or another health plan for the patient (coordination of benefits scenarios).</p>
<ul>
<li>This is where information for a crossover carrier such as Medicaid or Medicare is provided, if it&#x27;s applicable to the patient and the payer supports it.</li>
<li>For Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS) payers, Stedi returns an entry containing information about the patient&#x27;s home plan - the plan that actually verified the coverage. In this object, the <code>entityIdentifier</code> property is set to <code>Party Performing Verification</code>. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits#bcbs-home-plan" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The communication number referenced in <code>communicationMode</code>. It includes the country or area code when applicable.</p>
<p>Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code specifying the relationship between the entity and the patient. Can be <code>01</code> - Parent, <code>02</code> - Child, <code>27</code> - Domestic Partner, <code>41</code> - Spouse, <code>48</code> - Employee, <code>65</code> - Other, or <code>72</code> - Unknown.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The communication number referenced in <code>communicationMode</code>. It includes the country or area code when applicable.</p>
<p>Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying an organizational entity, a physical location, property, or individual. When set to <code>Party Performing Verification</code> for a BCBS payer, this is the patient&#x27;s home plan.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code specifying the relationship between the entity and the patient. Can be <code>01</code> - Parent, <code>02</code> - Child, <code>27</code> - Domestic Partner, <code>41</code> - Spouse, <code>48</code> - Employee, <code>65</code> - Other, or <code>72</code> - Unknown.</p>

rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies the external code list used to provide the specified procedure or service codes. Can be <code>AD</code> - American Dental Association, <code>CJ</code> - Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) codes, <code>HC</code> - Health Care Financing Administration Common Procedural Coding System (HCPCS) Codes, <code>ID</code> - International Classification of Diseases 9th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-9-CM) - Procedure, <code>IV</code> - Home Infusion EDI Coalition (HIEC) Product/Service Code, <code>N4</code> - National Drug Code in 5-4-2 Format, or <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].coverageLevelCode</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the level of coverage for the patient.</p>
<p>This will either be <code>CHD</code> - Children Only, <code>DEP</code> - Dependents Only, <code>ECH</code> - Employee and Children, <code>EMP</code> - Employee Only, <code>ESP</code> - Employee and Spouse, <code>FAM</code> - Family, <code>IND</code> - Individual, <code>SPC</code> - Spouse and Children, <code>SPO</code> - Spouse Only, or <code>Unknown</code>.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>CHD</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>DEP</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>ECH</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>EMP</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>ESP</code></div></div></div><div class="p-3 border-t"><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">View <!-- -->4<!-- --> <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The name of the <code>codeListQualifierCode</code>. For example <code>Mutually Defined</code> when the code is set to <code>ZZ</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies a specific industry code list. Can be <code>GR</code> - National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) Nature of Injury Code, <code>NI</code> - Nature of Injury Code, or <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined.</p>
<p>When this is set to <code>ZZ</code>, the <code>industryCode</code> property will be set to a place of service code.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>GR</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>NI</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>ZZ</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies a specific industry code list. Can be <code>GR</code> - National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) Nature of Injury Code, <code>NI</code> - Nature of Injury Code, or <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined.</p>
<p>When this is set to <code>ZZ</code>, the <code>industryCode</code> property will be set to a place of service code.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>GR</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>NI</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>ZZ</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The specific industry code. When <code>codeListQualifierCode</code> is set to <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined, this property will be set to a place of service code. Visit the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/medicare/coding-billing/place-of-service-codes/code-sets" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Place of Service Code Set</a> for a complete list of these codes and their descriptions.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating whether the benefit is in-network or out-of-network. Can be <code>Y</code> - Yes, <code>N</code> - No, <code>U</code> - Unknown, or <code>W</code> - Not Applicable</p>
<p>Code <code>U</code> indicates that it is unknown whether the benefits are in or out-of-network. Code <code>W</code> indicates that the benefit applies to both in and out-of-network providers.</p>
<p>Note that this property <strong>doesn&#x27;t indicate</strong> whether the provider is in or out-of-network for the patient. To determine that, you must check with the payer directly.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The specific product name or special program name for an insurance plan. For example <code>Gold 1-2-3</code>.</p>
<p>Payers are normally required to send the plan name when <code>benefitsInformation[].code</code> is set to values <code>1</code> - <code>8</code> and the <code>benefitsInformation[].serviceTypeCodes</code> contains <code>30</code> (Health Benefit Plan Coverage). However, behavior may vary by payer, so don&#x27;t rely on this information being present in the response. Note that the plan name returned in this property may not exactly match the name the payer uses in official plan documents or marketing literature.</p>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits#what%E2%80%99s-the-plan-name%3F" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">What&#x27;s the plan name?</a> in the benefits response documentation for more details.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b benefit type. For example, <code>7</code> - Anesthesia. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/send-eligibility-checks#service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Service Type Codes</a> for a complete list.</p>
<p>This list is specific to X12 version 005010, which is the mandated version for eligibility checks. It differs from the current <a href="https://x12.org/codes/service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">X12 Service Type Codes</a> list, which applies to X12 versions later than 005010.</p>

Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/send-eligibility-checks#service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Service Type Codes</a> for a complete list of codes and their names.</p>
<p>The word physician in service type codes refers to any healthcare provider, including physician assistants, nurse practitioners, and other types of healthcare professionals.</p>

size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the patient when they are a dependent. When the patient is a dependent, this array will contain a single object with the patient&#x27;s information. When the patient is a subscriber, or considered to be a subscriber because they have a unique member ID, their information is returned in the <code>subscriber</code> object, and this array will be empty.</p>
<p>When present, this object will always include the dependent&#x27;s name for identification, but many payers will also return the date of birth and other identifying information.</p><details items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>subscriber</code> or <code>dependent</code> level include missing or incorrect identifying information and that the payer was unable to locate the patient in their system. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Allowed actions you can take, based on the rejection reason code. For example <code>Please Correct and Resubmit</code>.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 first:border-t-0"><div><code>Please Correct and Resubmit</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Resubmission Not Allowed</code></div></div><div - Other Relationship.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">NPI</a>, tax ID, or EIN.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>provider</code> level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider&#x27;s NPI registration with the payer. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" the same patient into a unified record in the Stedi portal called an <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-searches-view" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">eligibility search</a>.</p>
<p>This property is for use by Stedi tools only, such as Stedi&#x27;s MCP server.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank"><code>AAA</code> errors</a> that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions.</p>
<p>Any errors that occur at the <code>payer</code>, <code>provider</code>, <code>subscriber</code>, or <code>dependents</code> levels are also included in this array, allowing you to review all errors in a central location. If there are no <code>AAA</code> errors, this array will be empty.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 This is either <code>production</code> when you send a request with a standard API key or <code>test</code> when you send a request in test mode with a <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/api-reference/index#api-key-types" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">test API key</a>. The <code>information</code> value is not currently used.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 <p>Instead of this property, we recommend using <code>id</code> to identify and track eligibility checks. 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>payer</code> level include issues with payer enrollment and that the payer&#x27;s system is down or experiencing issues. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The payer&#x27;s contact information.</p>
<p>Note that when <code>contacts[].communicationMode</code> is set to <code>UR</code>, the <code>communicationNumber</code> property may not contain a valid URL. Most payers provide a partial web address for their provider portal, or something similar, such as <code>www.example.com/portal</code>. You must add the appropriate scheme and separators, such as <code>https://</code> or <code>http://</code>, to make it a valid URL.</p><details pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The communication number referenced in <code>communicationMode</code>. It includes the country or area code when applicable.</p>
<p>Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Contains the dates associated with the subscriber and dependents&#x27; (if applicable) insurance plan. This information is used to determine their eligibility for benefits.</p>
<ul>
<li>Most fields contain a single date, but some can contain either a single date or a date range. Each field&#x27;s documentation specifies its format.</li>
<li>Fields that can contain either a single date or date range include: <code>plan</code>, <code>eligibility</code>, <code>planBegin</code>, <code>admission</code>, and <code>service</code>.</li>
<li>The provided dates apply to every benefit within the patient&#x27;s health plan unless specifically noted within a <code>benefitsInformation[].benefitsDateInformation</code> object.</li>
<li>If the payer sends back date(s) that are different for the subscriber and dependents, Stedi includes only the dates for the dependent in this object and omits the subscriber&#x27;s date(s). Dependents can have different coverage dates than the subscriber due to qualifying life events, such as starting a new job or passing the age limit for coverage through their parent&#x27;s plan.</li>
<li>Most payers return either <code>plan</code> or <code>planBegin</code> and <code>planEnd</code>, but the exact dates returned depend on the payer&#x27;s discretion and the patient&#x27;s insurance plan.</li>
<li>If the date of service is after the earliest ending <code>plan</code>, <code>eligibility</code>, <code>planEnd</code>, <code>eligibilityEnd</code>, <code>policyEffective</code>, or <code>policyExpiration</code> value, the patient likely doesn&#x27;t have active coverage.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation</a>, the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Strings</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-950/50 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-deprecated-pill">Deprecated</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Service Type Codes (STCs) related to the benefit type. For example, <code>7</code> - Anesthesia. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/send-eligibility-checks#service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Service Type Codes</a> for a complete list.</p>
<p>This list is specific to X12 version 005010, which is the mandated version for eligibility checks. It differs from the current <a href="https://x12.org/codes/service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">X12 Service Type Codes</a> list, which applies to X12 versions later than 005010.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">NPI</a>, tax ID, or EIN.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>provider</code> level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider&#x27;s NPI registration with the payer. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details benefits information in the response. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#provider-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Eligibility code lists</a> for a complete list.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 in the original eligibility check request. The response will always include either the subscriber&#x27;s name or member ID for identification, but most payers will also return the subscriber&#x27;s date of birth and other identifying information.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>subscriber</code> or <code>dependent</code> level include missing or incorrect identifying information and that the payer was unable to locate the patient in their system. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">NPI</a>, tax ID, or EIN.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>provider</code> level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider&#x27;s NPI registration with the payer. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A unique identifier for the eligibility request. It&#x27;s used to trace the transaction. Stedi always generates a trace number for internal tracking, and the payer may generate one as well. Stedi returns both its internal trace number and the payer&#x27;s trace number (if present) in this array.</p>
<p>You can&#x27;t set your own trace number when submitting eligibility checks through this endpoint.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 check request. the raw X12 EDI <a href="https://portal.stedi.com/app/guides/view/hipaa/health-care-eligibility-benefit-response-x279a1/01GS66YHZPB37ABF34DBPSR213" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">271 Eligibility Benefit Response</a> from the payer.</p>
<p>In some circumstances, this property may contain a <a href="https://portal.stedi.com/app/guides/view/hipaa/implementation-acknowledgment-x231a1/01HMRQV0N8SPHG58M4ZG1CRHH0" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">999 Implementation Acknowledgment</a> instead of a 271. A 999 indicates validation errors in the X12 EDI transaction, such as improper formatting or missing or invalid values.</p>
<p>If the 999 is returned in this property, many of the other response properties will be empty, as they are mapped to information in the 271.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div You can control the number of results returned per page using the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"children\":\"pageSize\"}],\" query parameter, which accepts a value between 1 and 200.\"]}]\n45:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"When there are additional pages of results, the response includes the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"children\":\"nextPageToken\"}],\" property. Repeat this process until the response doesn't include a \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"children\":\"nextPageToken\"}],\" property.\"]}]\n46:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"If you set the page size to a value \u003e 20, Stedi returns the requested batch check results in \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"children\":\"gzip\"}],\" format to reduce the size. bg-[#272626]\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L6a\",null,{\"items\":[\"cURL\",\"JavaScript\",\"Go\",\"Python\",\"Java\"],\"examples\":\"$undefined\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L6b\",\"0\",{\"name\":\"cURL\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L6c\",null,{\"label\":\"cURL\",\"source\":\"$6d\",\"lang\":\"bash\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L6b\",\"1\",{\"name\":\"JavaScript\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L6c\",null,{\"label\":\"JavaScript\",\"source\":\"$6e\",\"lang\":\"js\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L6b\",\"2\",{\"name\":\"Go\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L6c\",null,{\"label\":\"Go\",\"source\":\"$6f\",\"lang\":\"go\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L6b\",\"3\",{\"name\":\"Python\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L6c\",null,{\"label\":\"Python\",\"source\":\"$70\",\"lang\":\"python\"}]}],[\"$\",\"$L6b\",\"4\",{\"name\":\"Java\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L6c\",null,{\"label\":\"Java\",\"source\":\"$71\",\"lang\":\"java\"}]}]],\"data-testid\":\"request-tabs\"}],[\"$\",\"$L72\",null,{\"items\":[{\"type\":\"item\",\"value\":\"200-BatchEligibilityPolling_example1\",\"label\":\"Poll for results\"},{\"type\":\"item\",\"value\":\"400-default\",\"label\":\"Bad Request\"},{\"type\":\"item\",\"value\":\"401-default\",\"label\":\"Unauthorized\"},{\"type\":\"item\",\"value\":\"403-default\",\"label\":\"Forbidden\"},{\"type\":\"item\",\"value\":\"404-default\",\"label\":\"Not Error\"}],\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"200-BatchEligibilityPolling_example1\",{\"value\":\"200-BatchEligibilityPolling_example1\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$1\",\"200-BatchEligibilityPolling_example1\",{\"children\":[null,\"$L74\"]}]}],[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"400-default\",{\"value\":\"400-default\",\"children\":\"$L75\"}],[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"401-default\",{\"value\":\"401-default\",\"children\":\"$L76\"}],[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"403-default\",{\"value\":\"403-default\",\"children\":\"$L77\"}],[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"404-default\",{\"value\":\"404-default\",\"children\":\"$L78\"}],[\"$\",\"$L73\",\"500-default\",{\"value\":\"500-default\",\"children\":\"$L79\"}]],\"data-testid\":\"response-tabs\"}]]}],\"className\":\"hidden xl:flex xl:flex-col self-start sticky gap-6 max-w-[28rem] h-[calc(100vh-11rem)] top-[10rem]\"}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"7a:T4d7,Information about the patient's healthcare benefits, such as coverage level (individual vs. family), coverage type (deductibles, co-pays, etc.), out of pocket maximums, and more. \n \n Payers typically return at least the following properties: `code`, `coverageLevelCode`, `serviceTypeCodes`, and either `benefitAmount` or `benefitPercent`. However, the exact properties returned in this object are up to the payer's discretion.\n\nThe payer may send benefits information for service type codes (STCs) you didn't request - this is expected. The STC you send in the request tells the payer the types of benefits information you want, but they aren't required to respond with exactly the same STC(s) in the response. Receiving different STCs than you requested can also mean that the payer is ignoring the STC you sent, which is why we recommend [testing payers](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-stc-procedure-codes#test-payer-stc-support) to determine their support for specific STCs.\n\nVisit [Determine patient benefits](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits) for more information about benefit types, details about how to interpret the `benefitsInformation` array, and additional examples.7b:T465,Code indicating whether the benefit is subject to prior authorization or certification. Can be `Y` - Yes, `N` - No, or `U` - Unknown.\n  - When this property is set to `U`, it means the payer can't determine in real time whether prior authorization is required for the service. They may require additional details, like diagnosis or place of service to make a determination. Check the `additionalInformation[].description` property for any additional clarification. You can also contact the payer directly or use their provider portal to get more information.\n  - Some payers may send additional notes about prior authorization rules in the `additionalInformation[].description` property. Payers may also send prior authorization notes in a separate `benefitsInformation` object with a code of `1` (Active coverage), `CB` (Coverage Benefit), or `D` (Benefit Description).\n\nVisit our [patient benefits docs](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-network-status-authorization-referrals#is-prior-authorization-required) to learn more about prior authorization.\n\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.7c:T54b,Contains the dates associated with the subscriber and dependents' (if applicable) insurance plan. This information is used to determine their eligibility for benefits.\n- Most fields contain a single date, but some can contain either a single date or a date range. Each field's documentation specifies its format.\n- Fields that can contain either a single date or date range include: `plan`, `eligibility`, `planBegin`, `admission`, and `service`.\n- The provided dates apply to every benefit within the patient's health plan unless specifically noted within a `benefitsInformation[].benefitsDateInformation` object.\n- If the payer sends back date(s) that are different for the subscriber and dependents, Stedi includes only the dates for the dependent in this object and omits the subscriber's date(s). Dependents can have different coverage dates than the subscriber due to qualifying life events, such as starting a new job or passing the age limit for coverage through their parent's plan.\n- Most payers return either `plan` or `planBegin` and `planEnd`, but the exact dates returned depend on the payer's discretion and the patient's insurance plan.\n- If the date of service is after the earliest ending `plan`, `eligibility`, `planEnd`, `eligibilityEnd`, `policyEffective`, or `policyExpiration` value, the patient likely doesn't have active coverage."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"5f:[\"$\",\"$L4f\",\"application/json\",{\"value\":\"application/json\",\"children\":[null,[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"my-2\",\"children\":[[[\"$\",\"$1\",\"items\",{\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L50\",null,{\"constraints\":{\"default\":\"$undefined\",\"pattern\":\"$undefined\",\"format\":\"$undefined\",\"multipleOf\":\"$undefined\",\"minimum\":\"$undefined\",\"exclusiveMinimum\":\"$undefined\",\"maximum\":\"$undefined\",\"exclusiveMaximum\":\"$undefined\",\"minLength\":\"$undefined\",\"maxLength\":\"$undefined\",\"minProperties\":\"$undefined\",\"maxProperties\":\"$undefined\",\"minItems\":\"$undefined\",\"maxItems\":\"$undefined\"},\"deprecated\":\"$undefined\",\"name\":\"items\",\"namespace\":\"response\",\"schema\":{\"description\":\"Each eligibility check response is included as a separate item in this array. The response shape is identical to the shape of the response for the [Real-Time Eligibility Check](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility) endpoint, with the addition of two new properties that help you correlate the results with individual eligibility checks.\\n    - `batchId` contains the `batchId` Stedi returned from the [Batch Eligibility Check](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-batch-eligibility) endpoint when making the request.\\n    - `submitterTransactionIdentifier` contains the unique identifier for the eligibility check that you submitted in the request.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"batchId\":{\"description\":\"The `batchId` Stedi returned from the [Batch Eligibility Check](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-batch-eligibility) endpoint.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"batchName\":{\"description\":\"The name Stedi uses when displaying this batch on the [Eligibility check batches page](https://portal.stedi.com/app/healthcare/checks/batch). If you didn't specify a name when submitting the batch, this is the same as the `batchId`.\",\"pattern\":\"^[a-zA-Z0-9-_]{1,100}$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitsInformation\":{\"description\":\"$7a\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"additionalInformation\":{\"description\":\"A free-form message containing additional information about the benefits in the response.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"description\":{\"description\":\"A free-form message containing additional information about the benefits in the response.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"authOrCertIndicator\":{\"description\":\"$7b\",\"enum\":[\"N\",\"U\",\"Y\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitAmount\":{\"description\":\"The monetary benefit amount, such as a patient's co-pay or deductible. This value is expressed as a decimal, such as 100.00. \\n \\n The payer will always send a value in this property when the `benefitsInformation[].code` = `B` - Co-Payment, `C` - Deductible, `G` - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), `J` - Cost Containment, or `Y` - Spend Down. For those codes, this value represents the patient's portion of responsibility. \\n \\n The payer will **never** send this value when `benefitsInformation[].code` = `A` - Co-Insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility. \\n \\n Learn more about [patient costs](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits).\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitPercent\":{\"description\":\"The percentage of the benefit, such as co-insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility. \\n \\n The payer will always send a value in this property when `benefitsInformation[].code` = `A` - Co-Insurance. For this code, this value represents the patient's portion of the responsibility. The percentage is expressed as a decimal, such as `0.80` represents 80%. \\n \\n The payer will **never** send a value in this property when `benefitsInformation[].code` = `B` - Co-Payment, `C` - Deductible, `G` - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), `J` - Cost Containment, or `Y` - Spend Down. \\n \\n Learn more about [patient costs](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits).\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitQuantity\":{\"description\":\"The quantity of the benefit, qualified by the type specified in `quantityQualifier`. For example, `10` when the `quantityQualifier` is `Visits`.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitsAdditionalInformation\":{\"description\":\"Identifying information specific to this type of benefit.\",\"properties\":{\"alternativeListId\":{\"description\":\"The alternative list ID. This identifier allows the payer to specify a list of drugs and its alternative drugs with the associated formulary status for the patient.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"coverageListId\":{\"description\":\"The coverage list ID. This identifier allows the payer to specify the identifier of a list of drugs that have coverage limitations for the associated patient.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"drugFormularyNumber\":{\"description\":\"The drug formulary number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"familyUnitNumber\":{\"description\":\"The family unit number. This is returned when the payer is a pharmacy benefits manager (PBM) and the patient has a suffix to their member ID number that is used in the NCPDP Telecom Standard Insurance Segment, in field `303-C3` (Person Code). For all other uses, the family unit number (suffix) is considered part of the patient's member ID number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"groupDescription\":{\"description\":\"Group name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"groupNumber\":{\"description\":\"The group number for the patient's health insurance plan.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"hicNumber\":{\"description\":\"The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the [Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation](https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis), the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"insurancePolicyNumber\":{\"description\":\"The insurance policy number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"medicaidRecepientIdNumber\":{\"description\":\"The Medicaid Recipient Identification number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"medicalAssistanceCategory\":{\"description\":\"The medical assistance category.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"memberId\":{\"description\":\"The patient's member ID.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planDescription\":{\"description\":\"Plan name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNetworkDescription\":{\"description\":\"Plan network name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNetworkIdNumber\":{\"description\":\"The plan network identification number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNumber\":{\"description\":\"The insurance plan number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"policyNumber\":{\"description\":\"The patient's policy number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"priorAuthorizationNumber\":{\"description\":\"The prior authorization number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"referralNumber\":{\"description\":\"The referral number.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"benefitsDateInformation\":{\"description\":\"Dates associated with the benefits.\\n  - This is where you can find benefit-specific eligibility dates, if provided. These dates override dates provided in `planDateInformation` for this benefit type.\\n  - This is where the payer may specify the last time the service was rendered (`latestVisitOrConsultation`), which you can use to determine whether the patient has already reached the allowed frequency, if applicable. For example, this object could contain the date when the patient received their last dental cleaning.\\n  - These dates only apply to the `benefitsInformation` object in which this `benefitsDateInformation` is provided.\",\"properties\":{\"added\":{\"description\":\"Added date. Payers may return this information in the case of retroactive eligibility.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])(-\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01]))?$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"admission\":{\"description\":\"The admission date or dates.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])(-\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01]))?$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"admissions\":{\"description\":\"The date(s) for admission.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"date\":{\"description\":\"A single date.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"endDate\":{\"description\":\"The end date of a range.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"startDate\":{\"description\":\"The beginning date of a range.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"benefit\":{\"description\":\"The benefit date.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])(-\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01]))?$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"benefitBegin\":{\"description\":\"The date when the
