This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider's [NPI](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier), tax ID, or EIN.\",\"properties\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:responseProvider:properties\",\"type\":\"object\"},\"reassociationKey\":{\"deprecated\":true,\"type\":\"string\"},\"status\":{\"description\":\"Errors Stedi encountered when generating or sending the final X12 EDI transaction to the payer. These can include validation errors and payer unavailable errors that prevent delivery.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"submitterTransactionIdentifier\":{\"description\":\"The unique identifier for the eligibility check that you submitted in the original batch request.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"subscriber\":{\"description\":\"Information about the primary policyholder for the insurance plan listed in the original eligibility check request. The response will always include either the subscriber's name or member ID for identification, but most payers will also return the subscriber's date of birth and other identifying information.\",\"properties\":{\"aaaErrors\":{\"items\":{\"description\":\"When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more `AAA` errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the `subscriber` or `dependent` level include missing or incorrect identifying information and that the payer was unable to locate the patient in their system. [Learn more](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors)\",\"properties\":{\"code\":{\"description\":\"The error code.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":[\"15\",\"33\",\"35\",\"42\",\"43\",\"45\",\"47\",\"48\",\"49\",\"51\",\"52\",\"53\",\"54\",\"55\",\"56\",\"57\",\"58\",\"60\",\"61\",\"62\",\"63\",\"69\",\"70\",\"71\",\"72\",\"73\",\"74\",\"75\",\"76\",\"78\",\"98\",\"AA\",\"AE\",\"AF\",\"AG\",\"AO\",\"CI\",\"E8\",\"IA\",\"MA\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":{\"description\":\"The error description.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"field\":{\"description\":\"The error type, `AAA`.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"followupAction\":{\"description\":\"Allowed actions you can take, based on the rejection reason code. For example `Please Correct and Resubmit`.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:aaaErrors:items:properties:followupAction:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"location\":{\"description\":\"The location of the error within the original X12 EDI response.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"possibleResolutions\":{\"description\":\"Information to help you correct the error.\\n\\nWe periodically update this guidance, so these strings may change at any time and may differ between eligibility responses. **Don't build programmatic logic that depends on matching these strings exactly.**\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"address\":{\"properties\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:benefitsInformation:items:properties:benefitsRelatedEntities:items:properties:address:properties\",\"type\":\"object\"},\"birthSequenceNumber\":{\"description\":\"The number assigned to each family member born with the same birth date, such as twins or triplets. Indicates the birth order when there are multiple births associated with the provided birth date.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"dateOfBirth\":{\"description\":\"The member's date of birth.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"dateTimePeriod\":{\"description\":\"The military service date.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"dateTimePeriodFormatQualifier\":{\"description\":\"The format of the military service date and time period. Can be `D8` - Date or `RD8` - Range of Dates.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:dateTimePeriodFormatQualifier:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":{\"description\":\"Context that identifies the exact military unit. Used to report military service data.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"employmentStatusCode\":{\"description\":\"The member's employment status code, used to report military service data. Visit [Eligibility code lists](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#employment-status-codes) for a complete list.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:employmentStatusCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"endDateTimePeriod\":{\"description\":\"The military service end date.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityIdentifier\":{\"description\":\"The entity identifier for the subscriber.\",\"enum\":[\"Insured or Subscriber\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"entityType\":{\"description\":\"The entity type for the member. It can technically be set to `Person` or `Non-Person Entity`. In practice, our customers only receive `Person`.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:benefitsInformation:items:properties:benefitsRelatedEntities:items:properties:entityType:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"firstName\":{\"description\":\"The member's first name.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"gender\":{\"description\":\"Code indicating the patient's gender.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:gender:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"governmentServiceAffiliationCode\":{\"description\":\"The member's government service affiliation code, used to report military service data. Visit [Eligibility code lists](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#government-service-affiliation-codes) for a complete list.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:governmentServiceAffiliationCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"groupDescription\":{\"description\":\"Group name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"groupNumber\":{\"description\":\"The group number associated with the insurance policy.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"healthCareDiagnosisCodes\":{\"items\":{\"description\":\"Information about the patient's healthcare diagnosis.\",\"properties\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:healthCareDiagnosisCodes:items:properties\",\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"informationStatusCode\":{\"description\":\"The status of the member's information, used to report military service data. Visit [Eligibility code lists](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#information-status-codes) for a complete list.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:informationStatusCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"insuredIndicator\":{\"description\":\"Indicates the status of the insured. For the subscriber, this is always `Y`.\",\"enum\":[\"Y\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"lastName\":{\"description\":\"The member's last name.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"maintenanceReasonCode\":{\"description\":\"Code identifying the reason for the changes to subscriber identifying information, such as name, date of birth, or address. This is always `25`\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:maintenanceReasonCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"maintenanceTypeCode\":{\"description\":\"The maintenance type code. Used to acknowledge a change in the identifying elements for the subscriber from those submitted in the original eligibility check request. It can also be included when the payer used the birth sequence number from the original request to locate the subscriber in their system. This is always `001`\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:maintenanceTypeCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"memberId\":{\"description\":\"The member ID for the insurance policy.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"middleName\":{\"description\":\"The member's middle name or initial.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"militaryServiceRankCode\":{\"description\":\"The member's military service rank code. Visit [Eligibility code lists](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#military-service-rank-codes) for a complete list.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:militaryServiceRankCode:enum\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planDescription\":{\"description\":\"Plan name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNetworkDescription\":{\"description\":\"Plan network name\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNetworkIdNumber\":{\"description\":\"The network identification number associated with the insurance policy.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"planNumber\":{\"description\":\"The plan number associated with the insurance policy.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"relationToSubscriber\":{\"description\":\"The name of the `relationToSubscriberCode`. For the subscriber, this is always `Self`.\",\"enum\":[\"Self\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"relationToSubscriberCode\":{\"description\":\"For the subscriber, this is always `18` for Self.\",\"enum\":[\"18\"],\"type\":\"string\"},\"responseProvider\":{\"description\":\"Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider's [NPI](https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier), tax ID, or EIN.\",\"properties\":\"$5f:props:children:1:props:children:0:0:props:children:props:schema:items:properties:dependents:items:properties:responseProvider:properties\",\"type\":\"object\"},\"ssn\":{\"description\":\"The member's Social Security Number (SSN).\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{9}$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"startDateTimePeriod\":{\"description\":\"The military service start date.\",\"pattern\":\"^\\\\d{4}(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(0[1-9]|[12]\\\\d|3[01])$\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"suffix\":{\"description\":\"The name suffix, such as Jr., Sr., or III.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"uniqueHealthIdentifier\":{\"description\":\"The member's unique health identifier.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"subscriberTraceNumbers\":{\"description\":\"A unique identifier for the eligibility request. It's used to trace the transaction. Stedi always generates a trace number for internal tracking, and the payer may generate one as well. Stedi returns both its internal trace number and the payer's trace number (if present) in this array.\\n\\nYou can't set your own trace number when submitting eligibility checks through this endpoint.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"originatingCompanyIdentifier\":{\"description\":\"The identifier of the organization that assigned the trace number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"referenceIdentification\":{\"description\":\"The unique trace number assigned to the transaction.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"secondaryReferenceIdentification\":{\"description\":\"Identifies a subdivision within the organization that assigned the trace number.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"traceType\":{\"description\":\"The full name of the `traceTypeCode`. For example `Current Transaction Trace Numbers`.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"traceTypeCode\":{\"description\":\"The code that identifies the type of trace number. Can be `1` - Current Transaction Trace Numbers (refers to trace numbers assigned by the payer) or `2` - Referenced Trace Numbers (refers to numbers sent in the original eligibility check request).\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"tradingPartnerServiceId\":{\"description\":\"An ID for the payer you identified in the original eligibility check request. This value may differ from the `tradingPartnerServiceId` you submitted in the original request because it reflects the payer's internal concept of their ID, not necessarily the ID Stedi uses to route requests to this payer.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"transactionSetAcknowledgement\":{\"description\":\"The transaction set acknowledgment code provided in in the [X12 EDI 999 response](https://portal.stedi.com/app/guides/view/hipaa/implementation-acknowledgment-x231/01HRF41ES1DVGCA6X1EHSRPFXZ#properties.heading.properties.transaction_set_response_header_AK2_loop.items.properties.transaction_set_response_trailer_IK5).\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"warnings\":{\"description\":\"Warnings indicate non-fatal issues with your eligibility check or a non-standard response from the payer.\",\"items\":{\"properties\":{\"code\":{\"description\":\"The warning code.\",\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":{\"description\":\"The warning description.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"x12\":{\"description\":\"Typically this property contains the raw X12 EDI [271 Eligibility Benefit Response](https://portal.stedi.com/app/guides/view/hipaa/health-care-eligibility-benefit-response-x279a1/01GS66YHZPB37ABF34DBPSR213) from the payer.\\n\\nIn some circumstances, this property may contain a [999 Implementation Acknowledgment](https://portal.stedi.com/app/guides/view/hipaa/implementation-acknowledgment-x231a1/01HMRQV0N8SPHG58M4ZG1CRHH0) instead of a 271. A 999 indicates validation errors in the X12 EDI transaction, such as improper formatting or missing or invalid values.\\n\\nIf the 999 is returned in this property, many of the other response properties will be empty, as they are mapped to information in the 271.\",\"type\":\"string\"}},\"type\":\"object\"},\"type\":\"array\"},\"type\":\"array\u003cobject\u003e\",\"required\":true,\"nested\":false,\"children\":[\"$L7d\",\"$L7e\"]}]}],\"$L7f\"],[],null]}]]}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"61:[\"$\",\"$L4f\",\"application/json\",{\"value\":\"application/json\",\"children\":[null,[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"my-2\",\"children\":[[[\"$\",\"$1\",\"fieldList\",{\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L50\",null,{\"constraints\":{\"default\":\"$undefined\",\"pattern\":\"$undefined\",\"format\":\"$undefined\",\"multipleOf\":\"$undefined\",\"minimum\":\"$undefined\",\"exclusiveMinimum\":\"$undefined\",\"maximum\":\"$undefined\",\"exclusiveMaximum\":\"$undefined\",\"minLength\":\"$undefined\",\"maxLength\":\"$undefined\",\"minProperties\":\"$undefined\",\"maxProperties\":\"$undefined\",\"minItems\":\"$undefined\",\"maxItems\":\"$undefined\"},\"deprecated\":\"$undefined\",\"name\":\"fieldList\",\"namespace\":\"response\",\"schema\":{\"description\":\"A constraints.\",\"items\":{\"description\":\"Describes one specific validation failure for an input member.\",\"properties\":{\"message\":{\"description\":\"A detailed description of the validation This is returned when the payer is a pharmacy benefits manager (PBM) and the patient has a suffix to their member ID number that is used in the NCPDP Telecom Standard Insurance Segment, in field \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"303-C3\"}],\" (Person Code). For all other uses, the family unit number (suffix) is considered part of the patient's member ID number.\"]}]]\ncb:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"Group name\"}]]\ncc:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The group number for the patient's health insurance plan.\"}]]\ncd:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the \",[\"$\",\"$L40\",\"a-0\",{\"href\":\"https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis\",\"children\":\"Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation\"}],\", the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.\"]}]]\nce:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The insurance policy number.\"}]]\ncf:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The Medicaid Recipient Identification number.\"}]]\nd9:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"Dates associated with the benefits.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"ul\",\"ul-0\",{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-0\",{\"children\":[\"This is where you can find benefit-specific eligibility dates, if provided. For example, this object could contain the date when the patient received their last dental cleaning.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-2\",{\"children\":[\"These dates only apply to the \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"benefitsInformation\"}],\" object in which this \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"benefitsDateInformation\"}],\" is provided.\"]}],\"\\n\"]}]]\nda:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"Added date. Each field's documentation specifies its format.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-1\",{\"children\":[\"Fields that can contain either a single date or date range include: \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"plan\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"eligibility\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"planBegin\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-3\",{\"children\":\"admission\"}],\", and \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-4\",{\"children\":\"service\"}],\".\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-2\",{\"children\":[\"The provided dates apply to every benefit within the patient's health plan unless specifically noted within a \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"benefitsInformation[].benefitsDateInformation\"}],\" object.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-3\",{\"children\":\"If the payer sends back date(s) that are different for the subscriber and dependents, Stedi includes only the dates for the dependent in this object and omits the subscriber's date(s). Dependents can have different coverage dates than the subscriber due to qualifying life events, such as starting a new job or passing the age limit for coverage through their parent's plan.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-4\",{\"children\":[\"Most payers return either \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"plan\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"planBegin\"}],\" and \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"planEnd\"}],\", but the exact dates returned depend on the payer's discretion and the patient's insurance plan.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",\"li-5\",{\"children\":[\"If the date of service is after the earliest ending \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"plan\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"eligibility\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"planEnd\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-3\",{\"children\":\"eligibilityEnd\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-4\",{\"children\":\"policyEffective\"}],\", or \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-5\",{\"children\":\"policyExpiration\"}],\" value, the patient likely doesn't have active coverage.\"]}],\"\\n\"]}]]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1b3:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"Added date. For example, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"Child\"}],\" when the code is \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"19\"}],\".\"]}]]\n2d1:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"For the dependent, this can be \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"01\"}],\" - Spouse, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"19\"}],\" - Child, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"20\"}],\" Employee, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-3\",{\"children\":\"21\"}],\" - Unknown, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-4\",{\"children\":\"39\"}],\" - Organ Donor, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-5\",{\"children\":\"40\"}],\" - Cadaver Donor, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-6\",{\"children\":\"53\"}],\" - Life Partner, or \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-7\",{\"children\":\"G8\"}],\" - Other Relationship.\"]}]]\n2d2:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider's \",[\"$\",\"$L40\",\"a-0\",{\"href\":\"https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier\",\"children\":\"NPI\"}],\", tax ID, or EIN.\"]}]]\n2d3:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"AAA\"}],\" errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"provider\"}],\" level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider's NPI registration with the payer. \",[\"$\",\"$L40\",\"a-0\",{\"href\":\"https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors\",\"children\":\"Learn more\"}]]}]]\n2d4:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The error code.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":\"Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\"}]]\n2d5:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The error description.\"}]]\n2d6:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"The error type, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"AAA\"}],\".\"]}]]\n2d7:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"Allowed actions you can take, based on the rejection reason code. For example \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"Please Correct and Resubmit\"}],\".\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":\"Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\"}]]\n2d8:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The location of the error within the original X12 EDI response.\"}]]\n2ec:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The member's Social Security Number (SSN).\"}]]\n2ed:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The military service start date.\"}]]\n2ee:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The name suffix, such as Jr., Sr., or III.\"}]]\n2ef:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The member's unique health identifier.\"}]]\n190:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The location of the error within the original X12 EDI response.\"}]]\n2f0:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"\":\"Information to help you correct the error.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":[\"We periodically update this guidance, so these strings may change at any time and may differ between eligibility responses. \",[\"$\",\"strong\",\"strong-0\",{\"children\":\"Don't build programmatic logic that depends on matching these strings exactly.\"}]]}]]\n1a3:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The payer's Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services PlanID.\"}]]\n2f1:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The payer's contact information.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":[\"Note that when \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"contacts[].communicationMode\"}],\" is set to \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"UR\"}],\", the \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-2\",{\"children\":\"communicationNumber\"}],\" property may not contain a valid URL. Most payers provide a partial web address for their provider portal, or something similar, such as \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-3\",{\"children\":\"www.example.com/portal\"}],\". You must add the appropriate scheme and separators, such as \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-4\",{\"children\":\"https://\"}],\" or \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-5\",{\"children\":\"http://\"}],\", to make it a valid URL.\"]}]]\n2f2:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The contact information.\"}]]\n2f3:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The type of communication number provided.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":\"Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\"}]]\n2f4:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"The communication number referenced in \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"communicationMode\"}],\". It includes the country or area code when applicable.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":[\"Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-0\",{\"children\":\"5551123345\"}],\" for \",[\"$\",\"code\",\"code-1\",{\"children\":\"555-112-3345\"}],\".\"]}]]\n2f5:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The name of the contact person.\"}]]\n2f6:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"Deprecated; The payer's identification number for the entity receiving the benefits information.\\nThis shape is deprecated: This property is no longer used.\"}]]\n2f7:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":\"The entity identifier code for the payer.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-1\",{\"children\":\"Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\"}]]\n2f8:[[\"$\",\"p\",\"p-0\",{\"children\":[\"The entity type qualifier for the payer. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the \",[\"$\",\"$L40\",\"a-0\",{\"href\":\"https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis\",\"children\":\"Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation\"}],\", the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data.
